channel.title: Content to Upskill Yourself on Upskills
channel.description: Recent content in Content to Upskill Yourself on Upskills

item.title: 4 Science-based habits to build a highly productive brain| Jari Roomer
item.description: 4 Science-based habits to build a highly productive brain| Jari Roomer Most people reading this are - or will be - knowledge workers. In other words, they make money with their mind. And if you make money with your mind, you’re a mental athlete.

item.title: The Career Guide Principle | Sahil Bloom
item.description: The Career Guide Principles by Sahil Bloom Build a Personal Board of Advisors Swallow the Frog for Your Boss Create a Decentralized Growth Tribe Learn to Sell Prioritize Experience, Not Salary Avoid the Comparison Trap Find Your Zone of Genius Own Your Mistakes Get In “The Room” Build “T-Shaped” Knowledge References The Career Success Guide Forget Mentors: Employ a Personal Board of Directors Assembling Your Personal Board of Advisors

item.title: How to nail any phone interview | Ramit Sethi
item.description: How to nail any phone interview by Ramit Sethi References How to nail any phone interview

item.title: How To Write An Email Requesting Something | Ramit Sethi
item.description: How To Write An Email Requesting Something (& actually get it) by Ramit Sethi References How To Write An Email Requesting Something (& actually get it)

item.title: The “Tell me About Yourself” Interview Question | Ramit Sethi
item.description: How to nail any phone interview by Ramit Sethi References The “Tell me About Yourself” Interview Question

item.title: 24 Ways to Change Your Life in 2024 | Sahil Bloom
item.description: 24 Ways to Change Your Life in 2024 by Sahil Bloom Life Actions To identify what to prioritize in your days, use my Energy Calendar Technique. To improve your focus, turn on Grayscale Mode. To improve the quality of your outcomes, spend more time around the big thinkers in your circle.

item.title: The Letter to Your Future Self | Sahil Bloom
item.description: The Letter to Your Future Self by Sahil Bloom Summary by Sahil On January 1, 2014, I wrote a letter to my future self just before graduating. Ten years later, I opened it, and it hit me hard. Everyone should do this.

item.title: The Think Day: Your Secret Weapon | Sahil Bloom
item.description: The Think Day: Your Secret Weapon by Sahil Bloom Summary by Sahil In the 1980s, Microsoft founder Bill Gates began an annual tradition he called the Think Week. Gates would seclude himself in a remote location, shut off all of his communication, and spend an entire week dedicated to reading, learning, and thinking.

item.title: Crush Your Interviews with the Power of Storytelling | Dave Anderson
item.description: Crush Your Interviews with the Power of Storytelling by Dave Anderson

item.title: How to pass any first-round interview | Coach Erika
item.description: How to pass any first-round interview by Coach Erika in Lenny’s Newsletter The Career Whispers by Coach Erika - https://thecareerwhispers.substack.com/ Note: most of the valuable topics are covered in paid coverage. In my research, she has covered more in youtube (https://www.

item.title: Ramit's Brain Trust Insights | Ramit Sethi
item.description: Ramit’s Brain Trust Insights by Ramit Sethi This brain trust provides access to knowledge, wisdom and proven strategies that top performers uses in business, psychology, finance, marketing, and entrepreneurship. Copied content from 24 Things I Learned in the First Year of Ramit’s Brain Trust by Ryan Stephens

item.title: Scaling yourself during hypergrowth | Julia Grace
item.description: “Scaling yourself during hypergrowth by Julia Grace The higher up you go your job is sales People are not resources, they are people Resist the urge to solve non-engineering problem with engineering your job is to ask questions (good questions), not have answers recuit for the long long games, its a long term relations which only works.

item.title: A forty-year career | Will Larson
item.description: A forty-year career by Will Larson A career narative, spanning across 40 years(avg) for a person in tech industry. Highlights on pace, people, prestige, profit and learning. A forty-year career

item.title: Book: Be Your Future Self Now | Dr. Benjamin Hardy
item.description: Best Book: Be Your Future Self Now | Dr. Benjamin Hardy Be Your Future Self Now: The Science of Intentional Transformation

item.title: Toolkit - Annual Review & Planning | Sahil Bloom
item.description: Toolkit - Annual Review & Planning by Sahil Bloom Toolkit - The 2024 Annual Planning Guide Toolkit - The Personal Annual Review

item.title: Toolkit - Mental Time Travel: A Cheat Code for Success | Sahil Bloom
item.description: Toolkit - Mental Time Travel: A Cheat Code for Success by Sahil Bloom Summary by Sahil I have spent time with a lot of very successful people. I have observed two common trends: A struggle to appreciate the present and A struggle to identify the key actions to create the desired future.

item.title: Toolkit - The Magic of Character Invention | Sahil Bloom
item.description: Toolkit - The Magic of Character Invention by Sahil Bloom Summary by Sahil We all struggle with some degree of self-doubt and fear of failure. It’s particularly common among ambitious high-achievers, who, by definition, are constantly putting themselves in situations that are on the edge of their current competency level.

item.title: Toolkit - The Personal Annual Review | Sahil Bloom
item.description: The Personal Annual Review by Sahil Bloom Summary The end of the calendar year presents us with a valuable opportunity to reflect on the year that was and plan for the year that will be. It’s easy to glaze over the former and focus on the latter, but failure to reflect will eventually manifest as a failure to grow.

item.title: Toolkit - The Public Speaking Guide | Sahil Bloom
item.description: The Public Speaking Guide by Sahil Bloom Summary Confession: I am a nervous public speaker. But confident public speaking is a critical skill, so we need a set of strategies to increase our confidence and perform as the best version of ourselves.

item.title: Toolkit - The Ultimate Annual Review | Steve Schlafman
item.description: The Ultimate Annual Review by Steve Schlafman References Toolkit - The Ultimate Annual Review

item.title: 5 Rules That Will Drive a Long, Healthy Career | Nikhyl Singhal
item.description: 5 Rules That Will Drive a Long, Healthy Career | Nikhyl Singhal Summary Rule #1: Product manage your career like it’s a product. Rule #2: Ensure each transition is career additive. Rule #3: Whom you know will matter as much as what you know.

item.title: How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide | Ramit Sethi
item.description: How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide | Ramit Sethi Summary Key barriers to finding your dream job Barrier 1: You’re not getting specific Commit to defining exactly what you want Get specific in 15 minutes or less Barrier 2: Self-sabotage Barrier 3: Passivity How to find your dream job Identify what’s important to you in a dream career Choose three job roles Research each role Prioritize your job titles Naturally network FAQs About how to find your dream job Do the legwork, and get the job How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide

item.title: Three crucial skills that leaders must develop to become executives | Nikhyl Singhal
item.description: Three crucial skills that leaders must develop to become executives | Nikhyl Singhal Summary of executive skills Taking (almost irrational) career risk Learning to scale by trusting your team Developing advanced soft skills Must be humble Be highly self-aware Thirst for self-improvement Be able to hold things loosely so that you can quickly recalibrate and build the necessary skills.

item.title: Mental Models by Julian Shapiro
item.description: Mental Models by Julian Shapiro Summary First Principles One Level Higher Regret Minimization Pareto principle Theory of Constraints ICE (Impact, Confidence, Easy) Model References What are mental models?

item.title: Foundational Fitness Protocol & Stretching Protocols with Dr. Andrew Huberman
item.description: Foundational Fitness Protocol & Stretching Protocols with Dr. Andrew Huberman Summary Foundational Fitness Protocol PDF Protocol #1: Static Stretching Protocol #2: PNF Stretching Protocol #3: Antagonistic Muscle Group Training References Foundational Fitness Protocol Stretching Protocols to Increase Flexibility and Support General Health Hyperbolic Stretching

item.title: Optimize Your Water Quality and Intake for Health with Dr. Andrew Huberman
item.description: Optimize Your Water Quality and Intake for Health with Dr. Andrew Huberman Summary 8 oz (237 mL) of fluid per hour for the first 10 hours of your day Exercise, Heat and Hydration: The Galpin Equation: Body weight (in kg.) × 2 = number of mL to consume every 15-20 minutes Optimize Your Water Quality and Intake for Health

item.title: Toolkit for Sleep with Dr. Andrew Huberman
item.description: Foundational Fitness Protocol & Stretching Protocols with Dr. Andrew Huberman Summary Best nootropic: sleep Best stress relief: sleep Best trauma release: sleep Best immune booster: sleep Best hormone augmentation: sleep Best emotional stabilizer: sleep View sunlight by going outside within 30-60 minutes of waking.

item.title: (Non-Sleep Deep Rest) with Dr. Andrew Huberman
item.description: (Non-Sleep Deep Rest) with Dr. Andrew Huberman

item.title: How to get promoted at work | Steve Dennis
item.description: How to get promoted at work | Steve Dennis How to Get Promoted at Work

item.title: Reading
item.description: Appendix: Collection of Tech Skills Learn Tech Skills (Fast!) Marketing Examples The World Of Growth Marketing Become a smarter creator Making tech skills accessible - 100school Appendix: Course Start writing online in 30 days 100 day challenge for publishing more on Twitter - Closed as Twitter API changed.

item.title: Study Less Study Smart | Marty Lobdell
item.description: Study Less Study Smart | Marty Lobdel Don’t study for too long if you are not enjoying it. Take short breaks after 20-30 minutes. Don’t study in the living room, bedroom, etc. Study in a separate place dedicated to studying. Don’t try to memorize without understanding.

item.title: Elon Musk's 10 Rules for Success | Evan Carmichael
item.description: Elon Musk’s 10 Rules for Success Never Give Up Really Like What You Do Don’t Listen to the #LittleMan Take a Risk Do Something Important Focus on Signal over Noise Look for Problem Solvers Attract Great People Have a Great Product Work Super Hard

item.title: Taking Book Notes (How to Start) | Alex
item.description: What’s the best way to turn any book into a Second Brain of insights that you can grab off the shelf at any time? | Alex

item.title: How To Improve Communication Skills - 7 Unique Tips!
item.description: How To Improve Communication Skills - 7 Unique Tips! Keep it short and simple Be efficient with speaking; Remove all extra fill in words like actually, well, eh. Sounds more confident Pauses can be powerful and emphasise Use pauses to emphasise and make it powerful

item.title: How To Master The Art of Storytelling | Patrick Bet-David
item.description: How To Master The Art of Storytelling | Patrick Bet-David

item.title: How to MASTER the SKILL of COMMUNICATION!
item.description: How to MASTER the SKILL of COMMUNICATION!"

item.title: Storytelling - Become An AMAZING Storyteller!!!
item.description: Storytelling - Become An AMAZING Storyteller!!! Follow the 3 Part Format (Setup,Content,Resolution)

item.title: The magical science of storytelling | David JP Phillips
item.description: The magical science of storytelling | David JP Phillips

item.title: 3 Secrets To Brilliant Storytelling | Dan Lok
item.description: 3 Secrets To Brilliant Storytelling | Dan Lok Time & Location - be specific! Take them there. Be very specific! I.e. use dialogues. It puts them in the action. What’s the moral of the story? What’s the transformation and take away?

item.title: Best Personal Finance strategies | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: Best Personal Finance strategies | Ankur Warikoo

item.title: 10 Productivity tips and tricks that work | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: 10 Productivity tips and tricks that work | Ankur Warikoo Do not try to remember anything, document everything. I document everything, ideas, to-do list, meetings. Put everything on the calendar. I use the calendar to - block my time I schedule emails to myself and receive them in the future.

item.title: The importance of hard & smart work by Naval
item.description: The importance of hard & smart work by Naval Wrok Style: Sprint, rest and reasses -> marathon of sprints. Do thing right away, time is passing, getting older, do thing with full attention If you want to do something great, you need free time and free mind.

item.title: Time Management Tips for Students and Working Professionals! | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: Time Management Tips for Students and Working Professionals! | Ankur Warikoo Time is allocation Time is energy Time is money

item.title: Do the Important Before the Urgent
item.description: Do the Important Before the Urgent

item.title: 10 small changes that will improve your life | Matt D'Avella
item.description: Here’s the list. Which one do you want to work on next? When you set your alarm before bed, put it on the other side of the bedroom. Add one personal item to your work to-do list each day. Keep a “Praise” folder in your email inbox.

item.title: Goals vs. Systems
item.description: Goals vs. Systems

item.title: The Best Productivity Hacks of All Time
item.description: Productivity Hacks Pomodoro Technique Delegating The two minute rule The two day rule Time blocking The five minute rule Systems Daily highlight Batching Color coding To-do lists Consuming content at 2x speed Deadlines Eisenhower Matrix Goals Enjoying the journey

item.title: Your network is your NET WORTH
item.description: Networking tips Reach out; unless you ask, you’ll not get it. Ask for advice, mentoring, coaching. Engage with eagerness, wholeheartedly. Everyone has equal importance of time, so, use it wisely. Taken should make the process(for anything) easy for given, otherwise, giver will take back stage.

item.title: How to sleep productively
item.description: Sleep Facts An afternoon nap increases the brain’s learning capacity by 15-20% Sleep improves your memory, halting forgetting by 30-50%, relative to staying awake Not getting enough sleep increases sweet and salty cravings by 30-40% Sleep offers a three-fold bost in creativity and problem solving A good night’s sleep improves time to physical exhaustion when exercising by 10-30%

item.title: About Author
item.description: I am John Doe. And I can confirm that this is the best theme ever!
